name: Content Complete
description: Validates that all narrative content is written, visuals created, and components implemented. Checks page structure (ArtifactDetailWrapper, metadata, client component), CSS existence, and anti-patterns.

all_of.description: Next.js page with metadata, ArtifactDetailWrapper, and default export

validations.description: page.tsx must contain a default export and metadata definition
page.tsx MUST import and use ArtifactDetailWrapper with ESSAY_META (visual-essay-engineering-standard.md §2, Red Lines)
page.tsx should not contain TODO markers (indicates incomplete implementation)
Client component must NOT contain emoji characters — use SVG icons or CSS shapes instead

Client component must NOT contain raw Unicode escape sequences (\uXXXX) in JSX text content — they render as literal text, not as the intended character. Wrap in JavaScript expressions ({"\u0113"}) or use actual UTF-8 characters (ē) instead. See: JSX text nodes do not process JS string escape sequences.
Client component must import from ./images (images.ts) — all external image URLs must be centralized in the images file, not hardcoded inline
Client component must contain at least 3 elements — photorealistic essays require substantial photography
Client component must contain a Sources/Bibliography section — every essay requires source attribution at the conclusion

required_skills.name: Image URL Extraction
required_skills.reason: Production agent must use images.ts (created in G4.5) with centralized IMAGES constant. All tags must use src={IMAGES.key}, never hardcoded URLs. If images.ts doesn't exist yet, follow this skill to create it.

notes: G5 validates the core implementation files exist and have real substance. Standard convention: artifact_path = src/app/essays/{slug}/ (same dir as research). The CSS file is not strictly required because some essays use Tailwind-only. The Client component naming varies (e.g., TheWordRobotClient.tsx, GeographyOfWaterScarcityClient.jsx), so we validate page.tsx as the stable entry point. The contains_text checks enforce the ArtifactDetailWrapper requirement from visual-essay-engineering-standard.md. The regex_match check catches emoji usage, which is an anti-pattern for production visual essays. The JSX Unicode escape check catches a common bug where \uXXXX sequences are placed in JSX text content (between tags) instead of inside JavaScript expression containers ({"..."}). In JSX text, \u0113 renders as the literal string '\u0113' rather than the intended 'ē' character. The images import check ensures images come from the centralized images.ts file (verified in G4.5) rather than hardcoded inline URLs. The min_regex_count for elements catches essays missing photography. The Sources/SOURCES check ensures bibliography attribution is present in the rendered output.
